title: Updating the Project Initiation Document (PID) Throughout the Project Lifecycle
summary: Describes why and how a PID should be regularly revised when scope, resources, or risks change, keeping it a reliable source of truth for the project team.
content: Treat the PID as a living document that is updated when scope changes, resources shift, or new risks emerge. Keeping the PID current provides a single source of truth, ensuring all stakeholders have accurate project context, goals, and responsibilities without re‑creating documentation from scratch.
can_answer: Can a PID be updated after the project starts?
warnings: Neglecting to update the PID can lead to outdated information being propagated.
